question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R4 changes its Loopback0 prefix-SID from `index 4` to `index 3` — the
same index already used by R3's Loopback0. Two routers claim the same
SID in the SR Global Block. IS-IS detects the conflict and logs
%ROUTING-ISIS_SR_LSP-3-PFX_SID_CONFLICT. The conflicting routes are
rejected by the SR control plane; depending on tie-break, one router
"wins" and the other's prefix-SID label is not installed. Traffic
destined to whichever loopback loses (likely R4) cannot be SR-switched.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.104 (R4), the Loopback0 prefix-SID was changed from index 4 to index 3, colliding with R3's existing index 3 (both resolve to the same SR Global Block label 16003).
Mechanism: a prefix-SID must be globally unique within the SR domain, so two loopbacks claiming the same SID violates that invariant; the SR control plane resolves the collision in favour of one router and rejects the other's prefix-SID, so the losing loopback (likely R4's 192.0.2.4) gets no SR label installed and cannot be SR-switched - only plain IP forwarding remains for it.
Fix: restore R4's Loopback0 prefix-SID to index 4.
expected_symptoms_summary:
%ROUTING-ISIS-6-PREFIX_SID_INFO reported by R2, R3 (3 instances):
- R2: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16003 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.3/32 and 192.0.2.4/32'
- R2: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16003 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.3/32 and 192.0.2.4/32'
- R3: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16003 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.3/32 and 192.0.2.4/32'

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface Loopback0
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  prefix-sid index 3

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface Loopback0
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  prefix-sid index 4
